item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. it’s make a clever point, after all, antithiesm is basically just religon in reverse. If religon is 1 then antithiesm is -1, and just like religon it has various intensities, from casual to fanatic

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP already has a couple of crosslinks - one to an English SCP, and two to international SCPs (neither of which was ported to the English wiki recently.) However, there is still room to crosslink the mentions of the GoIs to their Hubs, to provide better context. (One of those GoI's hubs did not exist when this was posted in fact.)

Changing this:

Due to the behavior of and conditions necessary for SCP-3859's anomalous properties to manifest, SCP-3859's effects are particularly deleterious to members of the Church of the Broken God. Over 80% of Mekhanites have at least minor augmentations such as fingers which are capable of turning 360 degrees or fiber-optic scalp hairs. 5% of members, including Maxwellist Living Saints and Cogwork Orthodox Legates, possess near full-body augmentations.

To this:

Due to the behavior of and conditions necessary for SCP-3859's anomalous properties to manifest, SCP-3859's effects are particularly deleterious to members of the Church of the Broken God. Over 80% of Mekhanites have at least minor augmentations such as fingers which are capable of turning 360 degrees or fiber-optic scalp hairs. 5% of members, including Maxwellist Living Saints and Cogwork Orthodox Legates, possess near full-body augmentations.

And this:

Lacroix is now known to be a member of SAPHIR1 and had a key role in developing SCP-3859. A document recovered from their estate is below:

To this:

Lacroix is now known to be a member of SAPHIR2 and had a key role in developing SCP-3859. A document recovered from their estate is below:

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

Footnotes
1. La Société Athée Pour la Halte de l'Idéologie Religieuse, or "The Atheist Society for the Halting of Religious Ideology", a militant atheist group dedicated to wiping out the influence of religious organizations from the world. Their English branch is known as SAPPHIRE.
2. La Société Athée Pour la Halte de l'Idéologie Religieuse, or "The Atheist Society for the Halting of Religious Ideology", a militant atheist group dedicated to wiping out the influence of religious organizations from the world. Their English branch is known as SAPPHIRE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the internal logic of this: an atheist would have to be pretty militant to be able to believe in the paranormal yet not ever pause to reconsider their position.

item.title: Wow
item.content:encoded: This one was fun

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only two downvotes but still diablement underrated. Totally in the spirit of the GoI - hope SAPHIR will slowly infiltrate the -EN site !

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neat stuff. Creating connections between branches of the Foundation is great, a lot of good content is hiding out there. As for the two un-translated crosslinks, I started working on SCP-137-FR a while ago and this is definitely motivating me to finish it. I can also translate Grenat but as you mentionned, it might take a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will try to do it for you once i'm finished with the SAPPHIRE hub, 'cause that's indeed a very nice article.

Edit: SCP-137-FR as well as the SAPPHIRE hub had been translated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You made me feel sorry for Mekhanites?!

Take my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot better than the draft I read, and I especially like the very last line.

You do mention the Chicago Spirit appearing in 2014. Isn't the modern version the Chicago Spectre?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was wonderful, and I really liked that it was set in France with French characters as a homage to SAPHIR's parent site, though I admit I did see the 'SCP is activated by the religious fervour of militant antitheists' twist coming from the first mention of it affecting Lacroix.

The imagery in SCP-137-FR sounds pretty interesting too, would love if someone could do a good translation of it as relying on the janky google translate version makes me feel like I'm getting an imperfect version.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An excellent introduction of SAPHIR into the English wiki! Good on you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SAPHIR is a tortured backronym in the first place, translating the base and torturing it even more to get SAPPHIRE is perfectly in character for humans :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really adore the idea that these things were made to target Mekhanites, so the Foundation ends up more or less needing to help out the CotBG.

Also, SAPHIR's English acronym would be ASHRI, which I think sounds kind of neat?

My only criticism is that it's rather long; once you hit the "Foundation helps Mekhanites" and the source of the hookworms, I started feeling like the rest could be provided in a supplement or tale. The only extra bit that I felt was necessary was the "how dare they treat atheism like religion" because that's always fun. :)

item.title: Re: Ok, but I shouldn't need a translator to read all
item.content:encoded: Alternatively, translate the text and include it below, claim (accurately!) that the original document is in French and the Foundation has attached a translation? I mean, translation is still a lot of work, but presenting it as an anomalous document originating in France (or among French Canadians, I can't read French at all so I can't distinguish the two dialects) is perfectly acceptable (and kinda fun on a meta level)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You definitely delivered on the promise of the concept. Cross branch content is always a fun time, and this was certainly no exception. +1

item.title: Re: Ok, but I shouldn't need a translator to read all
item.content:encoded:
Ah, that's fair.

To be fair, the ones I chose would be a bit hard to translate— GRENAT_ tale, at the very least, would take several hours of editing the pictures therein to be in English.

item.title: Re: Ok, but I shouldn't need a translator to read all
item.content:encoded:
I mean that the crosslinks are entirely in french and at this moment doesn't have any translation of -INT.

And yes, I know that this is original and SAPPHIRE appeared before in 3380 (I translated to my native tongue)

It's more like a nitpick, but I liked the scp overall

item.title: Re: Ok, but I shouldn't need a translator to read all
item.content:encoded: I'm… not sure what you mean? This is original content, in English, based off of a French work. This is a really weird criticism.

item.title: Ok, but I shouldn't need a translator to read all
item.content:encoded:
It would be better if the works were translated in -INT instead of reading in French (i know that translator exists and all that, but it's better reading all in one language.

For that, no-vote

PD: Btw, I'm neither french or english native speaker

item.title: Bonjour! Ce commentaire de l'auteur sera entièrement en français. (Not really)
item.content:encoded:
So, this has an interesting story behind it— basically, about a week ago, the user Dr Lekter from the French wiki contacted me, saying that the French site had an interest in translating some of my works, as well as basing works off of the translations. I gave them the go-ahead, and immediately started looking for something I could translate in return.

I happened upon the Group of Interest SAPHIR, whose backronym is the least-weird thing about it. It's already been featured in SCP-3380, and on the surface, it can be described as a "militant atheist organization" dedicated to wiping out the influence of religion in the world, maintained on the French wiki primarily by DrGemini.

That is the tip of the weirdness iceberg.

SAPHIR is more than its simple premise— DrGemini has done amazing work with SAPHIR, including making up an Operations manual for the organization, and writing SCPs which include some rather out-there premises, such as the nine swords of Mohammad (Yes, that one) being the entire reason the Earth is round— SCP-281-FR, for anyone that's curious. In a word, this Group is Truly, Truly, Outrageous, and I fell in love with the concept.

Right as I was finalizing edits on this, I saw that DrGemini commented on the forum thread for this, so color me giddy. Glad you approve!

Thanks to all those who critiqued it, which includes but is not limited to OthellotheCat, UraniumEmpire, Wazikamawata, Mibu Asguard and Alan Stock. Thanks to Voct, Dr Lekter and DrGemini for helping me with French translation issues throughought my research on SAPHIR, and to Gemini for giving this their blessing.

Image derived from: https://pixabay.com/p-312696/?no_redirect

For those of you curious, I can't find an exact translation on the text (Latin is a tricky language), but if I wrote it right, I'm pretty sure SAPHIR's Latin motto reads "Fear our Luster".

Like my stuff? Check Out My Author Page for more!
